<!-- Pinned snapshot of https://wiki.pioneer2.net/w/Game_mechanics/Frame_data (raw wikitext), retrieved 2026-07-03.
     Source of authentic PSO:BB attack-animation frame data (30 fps). Parsed by scripts/extract-frame-data.mjs.
     Re-fetch deliberately and re-run the extraction if the community measures more data. -->
<small><div class="noexcerpt">< Return to [[Game mechanics]]</div></small>

''Phantasy Star Online Blue Burst'' runs at 30 frames per second. 
* ''Combo'' is the frame data when this attack is not the ending step of a combo. 
* ''Full'' is the frame data when the combo ends on this attack. 
''Heavy'' and ''Special'' are the same unless explicitly marked.

==Weapons==
===Combo frame data calculation===
Examples using HUmar frame data:
# Handgun, N: <code>N1 (Full) = 27 = 27f total</code>
# Handgun, NNN: <code>N1 (Combo) + N2 (Combo) + N3 (Full) = 14 + 11 + 19 = 44f total</code>
# Handgun, NH: <code>N1 (Combo) + H2 (Full) = 14 + 30 = 44f total</code>

===Frame data tables===
: ''Note that standard male animations are considered the 'default' animations, and all others are compared against it. If a category does not exist, this means that all the attack speeds are the same as the male animations. For example, female sword is the same speed as male sword, so it is not listed separately.''

|-
|Heavy
|48 (-4)
|29 (-4)
|37 (-2)
|22 (-2)
|52 (-10)
|}

==Techniques==
===Terms===
'''Casting Speed''' refers to the entire technique [[Techniques#Casting_Animation|casting animation]] from start to finish. Casting speed is composed of four animations: <code>Startup -> Charge time -> Cast time -> Endlag</code>
#<code>Startup</code> is the first movement your character does when you begin to cast a technique, and it is always 6 frames;
#<code>Charge time</code> is a stationary animation after startup that is accompanied by particle effects, and ranges from 0 frames (absent) to 15 frames;
#<code>Cast time</code> is the time from the end of the charge animation to when TP is deducted and the technique's effect happens;
#<code>Endlag</code> is the amount of time after the technique is cast that the player cannot move or perform any actions.
Casting speed varies between techniques, classes, and even specific weapons. Techniques in any context reach their maximum casting speed at or before Lv16.

'''Time-to-Cast''', or '''TTC''', is a combination of <code>startup</code>, <code>charge time</code>, and <code>cast time</code>. This is used for efficiency as there is no practical difference between these animations in-game (i.e. an interrupted technique will have the same effect regardless of which of these three animations was active).

===Casting Speed Tables===
Format follows '''XX<sub>yy</sub>''', where '''XX''' is the total Casting speed of the technique, while <sub>'''yy'''</sub> is the time to cast. The fastest casting speed for any given technique on a table is shown in <font color="ffff00">yellow</font>. There are 9 distinct casting speed sets. They are listed in order from fastest to slowest. 
{| class="wikitable mw-collapsible mw-collapsed"
|+ style="white-space: nowrap; text-align: left; min-width: 150px;" | 6 frames (male, unarmed, V801):

|<font color=f8e502>59<sub>24</sub>

|colspan="11" style="text-align:center" | -
|}

===Endlag Table===
Each technique disk has a set amount of endlag. Endlag decreases in increments of 5, and decreases in endlag for a given technique can happen at Lv. 6, Lv. 11, and/or Lv16 depending on the technique.

Minimum endlag for a technique is shown in <font color="ffff00">yellow</font>.

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:left"
|+ style="white-space:nowrap;" "text-align:center;" | Technique Endlag
|-
!
!Lv. 1-5
!Lv. 6
!Lv. 7
!Lv. 8
!Lv. 9
!Lv. 10
!Lv. 11
!Lv. 12
!Lv. 13
!Lv. 14
! Lv. 15
!Lv. 16+
|-
! colspan="13" |<center><font size="3">Attack</center>
|-
! style="text-align:left"|{{Foie}}
| colspan="6" |20
| colspan="6" |<font color="f8e502">15
|-
! style="text-align:left"|{{Zonde}}
| colspan="6" |20
| colspan="6" |<font color="f8e502">15
|-
! style="text-align:left"|{{Barta}}
| colspan="6" |20
| colspan="6" |<font color="f8e502">15
|-
! style="text-align:left"|{{Gifoie}}
| colspan="12" |<font color=f8e502>45
|-

|25
| colspan="5" |20
| colspan="5" |15
|<font color=f8e502>10
|-

| 
|- 
|}

==Photon Blast==
Startup for the first player is 61 frames. Players chaining will join the first player for however many startup frames were left.

Twins is 255 frames, all other PBs are 254. Two PB chains are 404 frames.

== HP Drain ==
{| class="wikitable sortable"
|+
!class="unsortable"|Weapon
!Frames
|-
!class="unsortable" style="background-position: center;" | !! style="background-position: center;" |
|-
|{{Sword|rare|Double Cannon}}
|style="text-align:center|25
|-
|{{Sword|rare|Girasole}}
|style="text-align:center|25
|-
|{{Sword|rare|Lavis Blade}} (first drain)
|style="text-align:center|20
|-
|{{Sword|rare|Lavis Blade}} (second drain)
|style="text-align:center|27
|-
|{{Sword|rare|Lavis Cannon}}
|style="text-align:center|21
|-
|{{Cane|rare|Gal Wind}}
|style="text-align:center|23
|-
|{{Sword|rare|Orotiagito}}
|style="text-align:center|22
|-
|{{Cane|rare|Psycho Wand}} (any technique)
|style="text-align:center|15
|-
|{{Sword|rare|Raikiri}}
|style="text-align:center|22
|-
|{{Sword|rare|Soul Banish}}
|style="text-align:center|23+1*
|}
: ''* Soul Banish drains on frame 23, but adds one additional frame to the collapse animation''

Total time to revive with a {{Tool|Scape Doll}}:

<code>(time to drain) + 7f (collapsing) + 119f (dead) + 70f (reviving) + 1f (standing but cannot act)</code>

Example: With Lavis Blade, it would be: <code>20+7+119+70+1 = 217f</code> total, or 7.23 seconds for a full retrap.

== Block Recoil ==
Blocking damage (EVP or DFP block) leaves the character in recoil for 8 frames.
<br>
{{Guides}}
[[Category:Guides]]
